steps.title: Add the equations to find x + y
steps.content:
Notice the symmetric structure of the coefficients: the 0.7 and 0.35 swap positions in each equation. Add both equations to combine them strategically:

$$0.7x + 0.35y + 0.35x + 0.7y = 1.75 + 2.45$$

$$1.05x + 1.05y = 4.2$$

Factor out 1.05:

$$1.05(x + y) = 4.2$$

$$x + y = 4$$

steps.title: Subtract the equations to find x − y
steps.content:
Subtract the second equation from the first to find another relationship:

$$(0.7x + 0.35y) − (0.35x + 0.7y) = 1.75 − 2.45$$

$$0.35x − 0.35y = −0.7$$

Factor out 0.35:

$$0.35(x − y) = −0.7$$

$$x − y = −2$$

steps.title: Solve the simplified system for y
steps.content:
Now we have two much simpler equations:

$$x + y = 4$$
$$x − y = −2$$

Add them to isolate $x$:

$$(x + y) + (x − y) = 4 + (−2)$$
$$2x = 2$$
$$x = 1$$

Substitute $x = 1$ into the first simplified equation:

$$1 + y = 4$$
$$y = 3$$
